title: Sun King

{{Quote|Totally mega cool!|Sun King|<ref name="tss" />}}
The '''Sun King''' was a powerful entity who, along with his siblings, controlled the [[Time/Legends|seasons]] of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] using the [[season scepter]]. His [[Gender/Legends|sisters]] were the [[Flower Queen]] and the [[Leaf Queen]], and his brother was the [[Snow King]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Snow King entered into an affair with [[Odra]], Duchess of Sleet, and had the [[Scepter/Legends|scepter]] stolen from the Sun King. The Snow King placed a large portion of the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] under perpetual [[Winter/Legends|winter]].

To avert the crisis, the Sun King and his sisters enlisted the help of the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Thanks to the efforts of [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and his friends, Odra was defeated, the scepter was returned, and the natural seasons were restored.

==Biography==
===The Odra crisis===
{{Quote|Okay, like, there I was, just hangin' out with some of the dudes […] We're like sittin' there just diggin' the sunshine… like how great it was to be out there baking, y'know? When, like, all of a sudden, ZINGO! Instant deep freeze! 'Whoa, dudes! Oh no! Hey!' The Iceheads were like, gone. History. And it's been snowing ever since!|Sun King|<ref name="tss" />}}
[[File:SunPalace-TSS.png|thumb|left|250px|The Sun King's palace, seat of the Sun King's power]]
The Sun King was, with his siblings, initially a prominent figure on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. At some stage during or before the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], the Sun King and his siblings came into possession of the [[season scepter]], a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-imbued relic that allowed the user to control the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] [[Time/Legends|seasons]].<ref name="coe">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref> The device was also a potent weapon. The Snow King set up residence in a [[Sun King's palace|palace]] in the lands near the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks']] [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. It was not a substantial structure, but it featured a large pool.<ref name="tss" />

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{EwoksDate|the appearance of the Sun King|The Season Scepter}}</ref> the Snow King was seduced by [[Odra]], the Duchess of Sleet. After entering into a tumultuous affair with the [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]], the Snow King's [[Heart/Legends|heart]] was [[Temperature/Legends|frozen]] by Odra, and under her influence, he sent his [[Icehead]]s to the Sun King's palace to [[Thief/Legends|steal]] the season [[Scepter/Legends|scepter]]. The Sun King, who was hanging out with a group of furry so-called "dudes," did not initially notice the theft, and when he did, his cocktail was frozen by the infiltrators. Frightened, he hid under his deck chair, so the Iceheads were able to escape with minimal fuss. Soon after, the [[Staff/Legends|scepter]] caused all of Endor to fall under a deep freeze.<ref name="tss" />

[[File:WeatherCabal-TSS.png|thumb|right|270px|The Sun King and his siblings, who worked together to defrost their brother's heart]]
The Leaf Queen contacted the Ewok [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] of Bright Tree Village for help. However, the elderly Ewok was absent, and the communiqué was received by [[Wokling/Legends|young Ewok]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]]. Warrick was unable to locate Logray, so he, accompanied by his friends [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]], flew to the Sun King's palace by way of [[flying leaf]]. However, Kintaka was apprehended by the Iceheads en route.<ref name="tss" />

===Skirmish at the Snow Palace===
{{Quote|Now, my brother the Sun King can have the season scepter back.|Snow King|<ref name="tss" />}}
[[File:SunKingScepter-TSS.png|thumb|left|250px|The Sun King with his scepter returned]]
The remaining three Ewoks arrived at the palace, where the Leaf Queen introduced herself and her siblings and explained the dilemma, with the Sun King recounting how the scepter had been lost in the first place. While the Leaf Queen continued to try and contact Logray, the Ewoks went to the [[Snow Palace]] to rescue Kintaka without telling the Leaf Queen their plans. They were able to infiltrate the palace, and although they were discovered by the [[Gender/Legends|Duchess]], they ultimately [[Skirmish at the Snow Palace|defeated]] and shrunk Odra, thawed the Snow King back to his original state, and freed Kintaka.<ref name="tss" />

The Sun King, his sisters, Logray, and Ewok [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] arrived after the battle, and the Sun King took back the scepter, complimenting the Ewoks on their actions. The Snow King returned Endor's weather to its natural state.<ref name="tss" /> [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Mankuskett]], [[Chair of Metagrowth and Polymorphism]] at the [[University of Sanbra]], described in a [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] paper that magic on Endor had turned "local kingpins into weather-controlling warlocks."<ref name="coe" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Bummer!|Sun King|<ref name="tss" />}}
The Sun King was an extremely laid-back entity, lazing on the Endor beaches with his "dudes," a word for which he had a fondness. He was an antithesis of his sister, the Flower Queen, who often fussed about affairs of state and her appearance, while he could not care less. He had [[Color/Legends|brownish-yellow]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and beige skin and [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="tss" />

==Powers and abilities==
The Sun King was able to use the powerful, Force-imbued artifact known as the season scepter.<ref name="tss" /><ref name="coe" /> His use of the scepter was powerful enough to change the climate of the moon, but his power was effectively nonexistent without it.<ref name="tss" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Sun King first appeared in "[[The Season Scepter]],"<ref name="tss" /> the thirteenth episode of the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], which premiered on [[November 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="Insider 27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref>
